The new models still show we are NOT YET DOING ENOUGH to address the climate crisis and secure a safe future for ourselves and our children.

We need to change faster, harder, stronger, and do better. 🤖
12/12

(See @doctorvive.bsky.social and @michaelemann.bsky.social article at top for detail.)

🏁 In short, we have made some progress, but we are still very far away from avoiding massive catastrophe. The results of the new modelling show we do have the power to bend the arc of this climate crisis of our own making. 
11/12

🛑 What HASN'T changed in the recent climate modelling? That’s perhaps MORE important:
Multiple scenarios, including the medium scenario that assumes current climate policies stay as they are, continue to predict CIVILISATION-THREATENING ☠️ changes to our planet. 
9/12

The output of the climate modelling has changed only because WE changed the inputs, by globally getting a move on with our climate action. We pruned a potential timeline where things could get even worse. But....
8/12

⚠️ The previous CMIP releases weren’t “wrong” ⚠️ 
They modelled a potential future path - realistic at the time - of practically no political intervention to prevent climate change. But since then we have taken enough action that the *worst* possible outcome is now not as bad as it used to be.
7/12

This worst-case CMIP scenario having a slightly-less-horrible outcome is good news! We’ve changed the future!
But of course people who are against addressing climate change need to distort that somehow. So they’ve invented this fib that the previous modelling was wrong. 
6/12

💪📉 What’s changed in CMIP’s most recent iteration of these projections is that, thanks to significant progress in addressing climate change, the “worst” scenario now has a less-worse outcome (in terms of degrees of warming by 2100) than it did in previous releases. 
5/12

These CMIP scenarios (there's a collection) project forward from our current situation of both climate observations and policy, modelling what outcomes are likely to eventuate based on a variety of different potential policy pathways into the future, and the effect of those on global warming.
4/12

What actually happened?

🧮 Here’s the (kind of) short version: A key resource in climate discussions and action worldwide is the “Coupled Model Intercomparison Project”’s scenarios (CMIP). 
3/12

🌏🤥💉 Here’s your climate disinformation vaccination for the week:
You may have seen that U.S. President Trump (among others) proclaimed that the UN had admitted its previous climate modelling was “WRONG!”
This is of course not true...
